meta.title: General Physiology I — Predicted Exam Questions (Repeaters)

behaviour.mcq: Select one option. Answer revealed immediately on selection.

questions.stem: When the diffusion rate of a substance across the cell membrane is directly proportional to the concentration difference across the membrane, the transport is:

options.D: Simple diffusion

questions.stem: The Nernst equation applies to the case in which:

options.A: An ion is cotransported with sodium
options.B: An ion diffuses by facilitated diffusion
options.C: The current produced by the electrical gradient of an ion is equal and opposite to the current produced by its chemical gradient
options.D: An ion is actively transported across the membrane

questions.stem: A basic characteristic of carrier-mediated transport is:

options.D: All of the above

questions.stem: The diffusion rate of a molecule across the cell membrane will increase if:

options.B: The lipid solubility of the molecule increases
options.C: The size of the molecule increases
options.D: Membrane surface area decreases

questions.stem: A sample of human red blood cells is placed in a plasma solution that has become hyperosmotic. The cells will:

options.D: Swell and undergo hemolysis

questions.stem: At rest, how does the inside of the cell membrane differ from the outside?

options.A: It is negatively charged and contains less Na+
options.B: It is negatively charged and contains more Na+
options.C: It is positively charged and contains more Na+
options.D: It is positively charged and contains less Na+

questions.stem: The positive shift (depolarization) of the cell membrane potential that coincides with channel 'inactivation' concerns:

options.D: An increase in Na+ conductance

questions.stem: The voltage-gated potassium channel:

options.A: Closes when the membrane potential reaches +10 mV
options.B: Opens instantly at the resting potential
options.C: Is open when the membrane is at rest
options.D: Is closed when the membrane is at rest

questions.stem: Which of the following explains the relative refractory period of a neuron?

options.D: Delayed closure of potassium channels

questions.stem: An inhibitory postsynaptic potential (IPSP) inhibits a neuron because it:

options.A: Decreases neurotransmitter release at excitatory synapses
options.D: Changes the neuron's threshold

questions.stem: Physostigmine causes stimulation at the neuromuscular junction because it:

options.A: Facilitates generation of the end-plate potential
options.B: Increases Ca2+ entry into the presynaptic neuron
options.D: Prevents acetylcholine from binding to postsynaptic receptors

questions.stem: In contrast to striated muscle fibers, smooth muscle fibers:

options.C: Develop tension more slowly and have a more negative resting membrane potential
options.D: Do not contain myosin

questions.stem: A motor unit consists of muscle fibers:

options.A: Of many types, innervated by several motor neurons
options.B: Of many types, innervated by one motor neuron
options.C: Of only one type, innervated by several motor neurons
options.D: Of only one type, innervated by one motor neuron

questions.stem: Slow oxidative Type I muscle fibers combine:

options.A: High myosin ATPase activity and low oxidative capacity
options.B: Low myosin ATPase activity and high glycolytic capacity
options.C: Low myosin ATPase activity and high oxidative capacity
options.D: High myosin ATPase activity and high oxidative capacity

questions.stem: Muscle strengthening is achieved by:

options.B: Activation of many motor units, testosterone, and exercise (all of these)
options.D: Activation of many motor units only

questions.stem: How does excitation-contraction coupling differ between skeletal and cardiac muscle?

options.A: Cardiac muscle contraction depends on extracellular calcium concentration, unlike skeletal muscle
options.B: Skeletal muscle contraction depends on extracellular calcium, unlike cardiac muscle
options.C: Cardiac muscle cells do not possess ryanodine receptors
options.D: In skeletal muscle, depolarization of the T-tubule membrane is not required

questions.stem: The destruction of red blood cells occurs mainly:

options.D: In the spleen

questions.stem: Erythropoietin is produced:

options.D: In the liver

questions.stem: Reticulocytes are found:

options.A: In greater proportion in the bone marrow and lower proportion in peripheral blood
options.C: In equal proportion in bone marrow and peripheral blood
options.D: In greater proportion in peripheral blood than in bone marrow

questions.stem: Which category of leukocytes mainly produces antibodies?

options.D: Neutrophils

questions.stem: Regarding monocytes:

options.A: They constitute 0-1% of leukocytes in adults
options.B: They do not have phagocytic properties
options.C: They have phagocytic properties and activate B lymphocytes
options.D: They do not activate B lymphocytes

questions.stem: Which leukocytes release heparin and histamine into the blood?

options.D: Eosinophils

questions.stem: In a normal blood sample:

options.A: White blood cells are more numerous than platelets
options.B: Eosinophils are the most common leukocyte
options.C: Lymphocytes are more numerous than neutrophils
options.D: Iron is found mainly in hemoglobin

questions.stem: Which statement about the complement system is INCORRECT?

options.A: It participates in innate immunity mechanisms
options.D: It consists of proteins

questions.stem: Which of the following does NOT belong to innate immunity?

options.D: The complement system

questions.stem: Which statement is true regarding antibodies (immunoglobulins)?

options.A: They consist of one heavy chain and one light chain
options.B: IgG are pentameric and constitute 20% of antibodies
options.C: Each light chain carries an antigen-binding region
options.D: IgM are produced in large amounts during the primary immune response

questions.stem: Hemophilia occurs with:

options.D: A sudden decrease in platelet number

questions.stem: The extrinsic and intrinsic coagulation pathways differ in that:

options.A: Only the extrinsic pathway can occur in vitro
options.B: The intrinsic pathway develops more slowly
options.C: Only the intrinsic pathway activates prothrombin
options.D: Only the extrinsic pathway requires Ca2+

questions.stem: Plasmin:

options.D: Acts during the initial phase of coagulation

questions.stem: Reduced blood coagulability is NOT observed in:

options.D: Liver dysfunction

questions.stem: The exclusive purpose of metabolism is:

options.A: Production of energy to maintain body functions only
options.B: Production of heat to maintain stable body temperature
options.C: Achievement of a positive energy balance
options.D: Production of energy and structural components for the function and growth of the organism

questions.stem: Body temperature differs from the usual reference (set-point) temperature when:

options.B: A very high environmental temperature prevails
options.D: The thermoregulatory centers change the reference temperature

questions.stem: Examples of thermoregulatory mechanisms are:

options.D: Shivering only
